title: Order lifecycle model
description: Orders move through pending, paid, shipped, delivered, and the terminal cancelled or refunded states.
body: Use when reasoning about valid order progression. An order typically flows pending → paid → shipped → delivered, with cancelled reachable before shipment and refunded after payment; cancelled and refunded are terminal. Do not allow a terminal order to re-enter fulfilment or a shipped order to revert to pending. Actions: model these as a directed graph and guard each transition. Validation signals include every persisted status change being a legal edge. Risk boundary: money-affecting transitions (refund) are advisory and defer to business.finance, and backward transitions need human review.
